mnemonics.title: GC Roots Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember GC Roots using acronym LLAJ: Local variables, Local references, Active threads, JNI (Java Native Interface) references

mnemonics.title: GC Algorithm Names
mnemonics.content: Serial = Single thread, Parallel = Many threads (throughput), CMS = Concurrent Mark Sweep (low pause), G1 = Garbage First (regions), ZGC = Z Garbage Collector (scalable)

mnemonics.title: Generations Order
mnemonics.content: Young → Old → Permanent (MetaSpace). Think: YOUNGER to OLDER to PERMANENT records - like going from temporary to permanent.

mnemonics.title: Mark and Sweep Steps
mnemonics.content: Mark = Find reachable (traverse from roots), Sweep = Remove unreachable, Compact = Move together (reduce fragmentation)

acronyms.usage: Automatic memory management in Java that reclaims memory from unused objects
The JVM component that performs garbage collection using various algorithms
Executes Java bytecode and manages memory including garbage collection
Programming framework that allows Java code to interact with native applications; JNI references are GC roots
A GC algorithm designed for low-latency applications, now deprecated in favor of G1
A server-style garbage collector designed for large heaps with predictable pause times
A scalable low-latency garbage collector introduced in Java 11
The region in Young Generation where new objects are initially allocated

visualTips.visualization: Draw a bucket divided into three sections: Young Gen (small bucket with Eden + S0 + S1), Old Gen (larger bucket), MetaSpace (separate container outside heap). Show arrows of objects moving from Eden → S0/S1 → Old Gen.
Think of a classroom: Mark phase = teacher marking attendance (who's present), Sweep phase = removing empty chairs (unmarked objects), Compact = reorganizing remaining chairs together.
Minor GC = quick cleaning of new items (like daily trash pickup), Major/Full GC = deep cleaning of entire house (less frequent, takes longer).
Picture a school system: Young Gen = Elementary (1st-5th grade), Old Gen = High School (6th-12th). Students (objects) get promoted after surviving certain number of years (GC cycles).

analogies.analogy: Think of GC like a janitor in an office building. The janitor periodically checks desks (heap), identifies papers (objects) that are no longer needed (unreachable), and recycles them to free space for new documents.
GC roots are like the main roots of a tree - everything connected to these roots (branches, leaves) is alive. Anything disconnected and not connected to main roots is dead wood and will be removed.
Memory leak is like a library where books are never returned. Even if no one reads them, they occupy shelf space indefinitely because the library card (reference) still shows them as checked out.

Generational GC is like a recycling center: frequently recycled items (Young Gen - cans, paper) are processed quickly and often. Long-lasting items (Old Gen - appliances) are processed less frequently but take more time.

Mark and Sweep is like a bouncer at a club: Mark = bouncer checking IDs and marking who's allowed inside (reachable), Sweep = removing everyone without a valid mark (garbage), Compact = reorganizing remaining people to make space.
